scenario: Vertex Bank runs a Camunda 8.8 loan triage process that handles 25000 loans per month. A job worker driving an ad-hoc sub-process needs to read metadata about every activatable inner element so it can choose which to start based on each application profile.
question: Which variable, created automatically when the ad-hoc sub-process is activated, lists the activatable inner elements with their IDs and metadata?

options.text: The adHocSubProcessElements variable, created in the sub-process scope, holds a list of activatable elements with elementId, elementName, documentation, properties, and parameters
The activeElementsCollection variable, created in the parent scope, lists the element IDs that were already activated by an earlier call from the responsible job worker
The bpmnElements variable, created at deployment time, holds the IDs and metadata of every element in the entire process definition for the worker to scan and filter
The outputElement variable, created when the worker completes its first job, stores the metadata about the inner elements returned by previous worker activations of the sub-process

a.text: Correct. When an ad-hoc sub-process is activated, the adHocSubProcessElements variable is created in its scope, containing each activatable element with elementId, elementName, documentation, properties, and parameters.

b.text: Incorrect. activeElementsCollection is an expression defined on the sub-process to return IDs to activate; it is not the special metadata variable created on activation for the worker.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no bpmnElements deployment variable. The activatable elements are exposed through adHocSubProcessElements in the sub-process scope at runtime.

d.text: Incorrect. outputElement is an expression that defines what to add to the output collection on inner flow completion; it is not a list of activatable elements.

explanation: Job workers handling an ad-hoc sub-process read the adHocSubProcessElements variable in the sub-process scope to discover each activatable inner element and its metadata before deciding which to start.
